column.name: record_type
column.description: Identifies the type of record represented in the row, such as an order transaction or a summary/target record. Analysts should use it to distinguish transactional sales rows from performance or planning rows when aggregating.

column.name: division
column.description: Business division to which the row belongs, with sample values such as BH. Use this to filter or compare performance across company divisions.

column.name: segment
column.description: Sales or market segment for the transaction, such as Retail. Analysts can use this to separate retail, wholesale, or other route-to-market views if present.

column.name: order_date
column.description: Date and time when the order was placed or recorded. This is the primary transaction date for daily sales and activity analysis.

column.name: business_month
column.description: Accounting or business month associated with the row, typically in YYYY-MM format. Use this for monthly reporting and target achievement calculations rather than deriving month from dates if business calendars differ.

column.name: report_date
column.description: Date for which the row is reported in the dashboard or reporting layer. It may match the order date for transactional rows and is useful for daily snapshots or cut-off based reporting.

column.name: uuid
column.description: Composite unique identifier tying the employee or position context to the record, often combining division, position, employee name, and contact details. Use cautiously as a technical/business key rather than a clean employee ID.

column.name: position_code
column.description: Code representing the employee's sales position or territory assignment, such as BH_DEL_FF3. Analysts can use it to map performance to field roles or geographic territories.

column.name: emp_id
column.description: Unique employee identifier for the salesperson or field employee associated with the record. This is the preferred key for joining employee master data and tracking individual performance.

column.name: emp_name
column.description: Name of the employee responsible for the outlet, beat, or order. Use for readable reporting, while emp_id should be used for joins and de-duplication.

column.name: designation
column.description: Job title or role of the employee, such as TSO. Analysts can use this to group performance by sales hierarchy level or role type.

column.name: tl_name
column.description: Name of the employee's team leader or immediate reporting manager. Useful for rollups and performance analysis at team-leader level.

column.name: tl_designation
column.description: Designation of the team leader, such as TSM. Use with tl_name to understand the manager's organizational level.

column.name: rsm_name
column.description: Name of the Regional Sales Manager associated with the employee or territory. Analysts can use it for regional hierarchy reporting and accountability.

column.name: rsm_designation
column.description: Designation of the regional manager, typically RSM. This helps validate hierarchy level and role-based aggregations.

column.name: asm_name
column.description: Name of the Area Sales Manager or equivalent manager linked to the employee. Use this for area-level rollups and management reporting.

column.name: asm_designation
column.description: Designation of the area manager or equivalent supervisor. Analysts should use it to interpret the level of asm_name in the sales hierarchy.

column.name: emp_zone
column.description: Geographic zone assigned to the employee, such as North. Use it for employee-territory based regional analysis.

column.name: emp_state
column.description: State assigned to the employee's working territory, such as Delhi. This may differ from client_state and should be interpreted as the employee territory state.

column.name: emp_city
column.description: City assigned to the employee's working location or territory. Use this for field-force geographic analysis.

column.name: hq_state
column.description: Headquarter state associated with the employee or sales position. Analysts can use it to map employees to their official base location.

column.name: emp_type
column.description: Employee type or employment classification when available, such as permanent, contract, or similar categories. Null values indicate the classification was not provided in the source row.

column.name: state_group
column.description: Higher-level grouping of states used for internal reporting or sales planning. Null indicates no state group was assigned or applicable for the row.

column.name: is_active
column.description: Flag indicating whether the employee is active in the organization or sales system. Analysts should use this to include/exclude inactive employees in current performance reporting when populated.

column.name: is_field_employee
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the employee is part of the field sales force. True values identify employees directly involved in field sales or outlet servicing.

column.name: deactive_date
column.description: Date on which the employee was deactivated, if applicable. Null generally means the employee is active or no deactivation date is recorded.

column.name: client_id
column.description: Unique identifier for the outlet, retailer, or customer associated with the record. This is the preferred key for outlet-level joins, deduplication, and universe analysis.

column.name: client_name
column.description: Name of the outlet, retailer, or customer, such as Royal beauty. Use for reporting labels, while client_id should be used for accurate aggregation.

column.name: client_type
column.description: Type of customer or channel participant, such as Retailer. Analysts can use this to split sales and coverage metrics by outlet or customer category.

column.name: contact_type
column.description: Business category or outlet specialization, such as Cosmetics. This helps classify outlets by trade type or relevant product category.

column.name: client_zone
column.description: Geographic zone of the client or outlet, such as North. Use it for customer-location based regional analysis, which may differ from employee zone.

column.name: client_state
column.description: State where the client or outlet is located. This is the customer geography and should be used for market-location reporting.

column.name: client_city
column.description: City or local market where the client or outlet is located, such as East Delhi. Use it for city-level outlet and sales analysis.

column.name: is_in_universe
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the outlet is part of the defined sales universe or target outlet base. True outlets should generally be included in coverage and productivity denominator calculations.

column.name: is_new_outlet
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the outlet is newly added during the relevant period. Use it to analyze new outlet acquisition and sales from recently onboarded outlets.

column.name: product_id
column.description: Unique internal identifier for the product. Use this as the stable product-level key for joins and product performance analysis.

column.name: variant_id
column.description: Unique identifier for the product variant or SKU-level variation. This is useful for shade, size, pack, or variant-level analysis.

column.name: product_code
column.description: Product code used in business or ERP systems, often numeric and sometimes stored as text. Analysts can use it as an alternate product key but should watch for formatting such as decimal suffixes.

column.name: vsku_code
column.description: Variant SKU code representing the sellable SKU or variant. Use this for SKU-level sales reporting and mapping to product masters.

column.name: product_name
column.description: Name of the product family or item, such as Fashion Sindoor. It provides a human-readable product label for reports.

column.name: variant_name
column.description: Name of the specific product variant, such as color, size, or shade. Use this for SKU/variant-level reporting where product_name is not sufficiently granular.

column.name: category
column.description: Top-level product category, such as SINDOOR or FACE. Analysts can use it for category sales, mix, and target tracking.

column.name: subcategory
column.description: More granular product classification within the category, such as COMPACT. Use it to analyze product performance below category level.

column.name: product_category
column.description: Business-defined product grouping or product line, such as Shringarika Powder Sindoor. This may be the preferred commercial grouping for brand or portfolio analysis.

column.name: mrp
column.description: Maximum Retail Price of the product or SKU. Analysts should treat it as the listed consumer price, not necessarily the invoiced or realized sales value.

column.name: is_npd
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the product is a new product development/new launch item. Use it to isolate NPD contribution and launch performance.

column.name: distributer_name
column.description: Name of the distributor fulfilling or associated with the order. Use it to analyze sales by distributor and territory servicing partner.

column.name: distributer_type
column.description: Type of distributor or channel partner, such as Distributor. This helps classify supply-side partners in channel reporting.

column.name: source_name
column.description: Name of the source entity, system, or upstream partner when applicable. Null values indicate no separate source was captured for the row.

column.name: source_type
column.description: Type or classification of the source entity when applicable. Use it with source_name to distinguish data or channel sources where populated.

column.name: ss_id
column.description: Identifier for the super stockist or secondary stockist, if applicable. Null indicates the order was not linked to a recorded SS entity in the row.

column.name: beat_name
column.description: Name of the beat or planned sales route associated with the outlet visit/order, such as Lal Quarter Dl. Analysts can use it for route productivity and outlet coverage analysis.

column.name: quantity
column.description: Number of units or pieces ordered/sold for the product variant in the row. Sum this for volume analysis at SKU, outlet, employee, or geography level.

column.name: gross_value
column.description: Gross sales value for the line before net value adjustments, discounts, taxes, or other internal deductions depending on business rules. Use it for top-line order value analysis.

column.name: p3_val
column.description: Sales value assigned to the internal P3 bucket or sales classification for the row. Analysts should aggregate it according to business definitions of P3 value and compare with p3_nsv for net value analysis.

column.name: p3_nsv
column.description: Net Sales Value associated with the P3 bucket after applicable deductions or conversion from gross value. This is typically a preferred value measure for P3 revenue performance.

column.name: p2_val
column.description: Sales value assigned to the internal P2 bucket or sales classification for the row. It may be zero where the transaction is not classified under P2.

column.name: p2_nsv
column.description: Net Sales Value associated with the P2 bucket after applicable deductions. Use it for P2 revenue performance where the business tracks multiple sales buckets.

column.name: tc
column.description: Total call indicator or count for the row, generally used in outlet visit/activity metrics. Analysts should aggregate carefully to avoid double-counting across multiple product lines for the same outlet call.

column.name: pc
column.description: Productive call indicator or count showing whether the call/order contributed to productivity, often 1 for a productive outlet and 0 otherwise. Use for productivity calculations, with attention to line-level duplication.

column.name: tlsd
column.description: Total lines sold indicator/count for the row, representing product-line sales activity. It is commonly used to count selling lines or assortment breadth in an order.

column.name: ulsd
column.description: Unique lines sold indicator/count for the row, used to count distinct productive lines or SKUs without duplication under the relevant grain. Analysts should validate aggregation grain before summing.

column.name: eco
column.description: Effective coverage or effective call indicator, typically 1 when the outlet contributes to effective coverage for the period and 0 otherwise. Use it for coverage productivity metrics, avoiding duplicate counts across SKU rows.

column.name: mtd_tc
column.description: Month-to-date total calls for the relevant employee, outlet, territory, or reporting grain. Null values indicate the MTD metric is not populated for that row type.

column.name: mtd_pc
column.description: Month-to-date productive calls for the relevant reporting grain. Use it to monitor cumulative productivity within the business month.

column.name: mtd_tlsd
column.description: Month-to-date total lines sold for the relevant reporting grain. Analysts can use it to track cumulative line-selling activity across the month.

column.name: mtd_ulsd
column.description: Month-to-date unique lines sold for the relevant reporting grain. Use it for cumulative assortment or SKU breadth analysis where populated.

column.name: mtd_eco
column.description: Month-to-date effective coverage count or indicator at the relevant reporting grain. It supports cumulative coverage tracking versus targets.

column.name: mtd_p3_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P3 Net Sales Value for the relevant reporting grain. Use it for cumulative P3 revenue tracking during the business month.

column.name: mtd_p2_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P2 Net Sales Value for the relevant reporting grain. Use it for cumulative P2 revenue tracking during the business month.

column.name: universe
column.description: Count or size of the defined outlet universe for the relevant employee, territory, or reporting grain. It is typically used as the denominator for coverage and productivity metrics.

column.name: lppc
column.description: Lines per productive call, a productivity metric measuring selling breadth per productive call. Higher values generally indicate better assortment selling or more SKUs sold per productive outlet.

column.name: productivity_pct
column.description: Productivity percentage, typically productive calls divided by total calls or a related business-defined productivity ratio. Analysts should interpret it as a performance KPI and confirm the exact formula in business rules.

column.name: pc_target
column.description: Target number of productive calls for the period and reporting grain. Use it as the denominator or benchmark for pc_achievement_pct.

column.name: lppc_target
column.description: Target value for lines per productive call. Compare lppc against this field to assess assortment-selling productivity performance.

column.name: p3_target_nsv
column.description: Target Net Sales Value for the P3 bucket for the relevant period and reporting grain. Use it as the benchmark for P3 sales achievement.

column.name: p2_target_nsv
column.description: Target Net Sales Value for the P2 bucket for the relevant period and reporting grain. Use it as the benchmark for P2 sales achievement.

column.name: focus_target_vol
column.description: Volume target for focus products, categories, or initiatives. Use it to evaluate whether prioritized products are achieving planned quantity objectives.

column.name: focus_target_eco
column.description: Effective coverage target for focus products or initiatives. It measures expected outlet coverage for focus assortment rather than total business alone.

column.name: pc_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against the productive call target. Values are typically calculated as actual productive calls divided by pc_target, expressed as a percentage.

column.name: p3_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against the P3 NSV target. Use it to evaluate progress toward P3 revenue goals for the reporting period.

column.name: p2_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against the P2 NSV target. Use it to evaluate progress toward P2 revenue goals for the reporting period.

column.name: new_outlet_count
column.description: Count of newly added outlets for the relevant reporting grain and period. Use it to track outlet expansion and new customer acquisition.

column.name: new_outlet_p3_nsv
column.description: P3 Net Sales Value generated from new outlets. Analysts can use it to measure revenue contribution from newly onboarded outlets.
